title: activity — regulatory compliance
description: Compliance controls this module produces evidence for. Activity is the user-facing feed lane — its compliance surface is user-oriented (right of access, right to erasure, right to know) rather than regulator-oriented (that's audit's job). GDPR Art. 15 is satisfied by the /activities/mine endpoint; Art. 17 cascades on Identity erasure; CCPA §1798.100 right to know is satisfied by the feed itself.

controls.evidence: Activity capture is transparent — every logged event is visible to the user via /activities/mine + the tenant feed. The consent-gate for activity_digest_email means marketing-consent-only users can browse the feed but do not receive email digests.

Activity stores only what the domain model's `logOnly()` declares — the developer picks the minimum set of fields. Descriptions are human-readable narrative; raw payloads MUST NOT be logged. Enforced by code review (never enforced structurally — the vendor allows arbitrary properties). Retention is short (90d default; up to 730d only for regulator holds).

retention.json — 90d default; 365d extended tier; 730d ceiling. PurgeExpiredActivitiesJob runs nightly; ArchiveActivitiesToColdStorageJob rotates rows out of hot storage at 90d. ActivityPurgeCompletedNotification tells tenant admins what was purged (transparency requirement).

The /api/v1/activities/mine endpoint returns every activity where the caller is causer OR subject. Fulfils the tenant-user's right to see their own personal-data footprint on the platform without a DPO ticket. Also feeds the standard DSAR export via `activity:export --user=<subject_id>`.

PurgeActivitiesForErasedIdentity listener on user::IdentityErased hard-deletes every activity row where the erased identity is causer OR subject. Unlike audit (which anonymises), activity purges outright — activity rows are user-facing narrative and preserving them would leak the erased identity's actions on other users' feeds.

Two-lane split. Activity (this module) is user-facing feed; sensitive personal data belongs in the audit lane which encrypts fields at rest. Consumer contract in readme.md §8 documents 'NEVER `LogsActivity` on PII-heavy aggregates' — enforced by code review + the standards-steward auditor agent.

Encrypted at rest via Postgres TDE. Every read logged via monitoring metrics. Access-scoped via activity.enforce_scope middleware — the caller sees only activities on entities they hold viewing permissions for.
ACTIVITY_CROSS_TENANT_READ error (defense-in-depth against tenant-isolation breach attempts) fires the security-incident notification pipeline. Should be zero-occurrence at steady state.

The /activities/mine feed is the tenant-user's real-time answer to 'what data does the platform hold about me + what has been done with my data'. Satisfies the right to know without a formal DSAR request.
Same as GDPR Art. 17. PurgeActivitiesForErasedIdentity listener handles cascade.
The activity feed carries `properties` context — the exact facility, exact team, exact booking details of every event. Satisfies specific-information disclosure.

Retention policy CRUD is captured via HasAudit on ActivityRetentionPolicy — every override is regulator-relevant evidence. override_reason column is REQUIRED, min 20 chars, retained indefinitely on the audit stream.
Feed read + retention-policy write endpoints require TLS 1.2+ (Sanctum PATs). Every mutation on the retention policy is signed by the auth_user PAT and captured on audit.

health.json + metrics.json cover the module's operational surface — write latency, purge volume, orphan-batch count, backfill progress. Alerts fire on staleness (backfill still running past 48h) + orphan-batch spikes (indicates request crashes upstream).

activity.enforce_scope middleware enforces subject-permission-narrowed reads — a caller sees activity only on entities they hold viewing permissions for. Delegates to the entity's own policy via ActivityPolicy@viewByEntity.

PurgeExpiredActivitiesJob nightly; ArchiveActivitiesToColdStorageJob weekly. Cold-storage rotation ensures rows past hot-retention are still retrievable (for extended-tier tenants) until the ultimate retention_days limit.

Consumer contract prohibits LogsActivity on PII-heavy aggregates. Descriptions are user-readable narrative — no raw payload dumps. `logOnly()` guidance in the trait documentation directs authors to whitelist minimum fields.

hipaa.note: Activity capture on HIPAA-adjacent aggregates (athlete medical clearances, athlete injuries) is FORBIDDEN by the consumer contract — those aggregates use audit instead. The activity module itself does not touch HIPAA data by design.

controls.evidence: Activity rows attributed to minor subjects (Athletes under 13) inherit the tighter minor-retention policy from the compliance module. The retention_policy defaults to 90d and cannot be extended without documenting the guardian-consent basis in the override_reason.

The feed uses semantic HTML (list + list items) with ARIA landmarks. Every activity row exposes causer + subject + timestamp as separate accessible fields — screen readers announce them in a scannable order.
Digest email links carry entity names in the CTA — 'View Court 3's activity' is unambiguous out of context.
